title: deferred playbook executor with scheduled dry-run and safe rollback hooks
preview: Motivation: On-call automation needs a way to schedule runbooks to execute outside peak windows, run a non-destructive dry-run before live execution, and ensure safe automatic rollback when automation makes state changes. This PR adds a Deferred Playbook Executor that accepts scheduled windows and optional dry-run p...
text: Motivation: On-call automation needs a way to schedule runbooks to execute outside peak windows, run a non-destructive dry-run before live execution, and ensure safe automatic rollback when automation makes state changes. This PR adds a Deferred Playbook Executor that accepts scheduled windows and optional dry-run payloads, a scheduled dry-run runner that simulates playbook steps without side effects, and pluggable safe-rollback hooks that capture pre-change snapshots and provide an automated revert path. Key design points: (1) All deferred executions are stored as immutable scheduling tasks with idempotency tokens, (2) dry-run mode routes actions to a simulator layer that returns predicted outcomes and a list of mutated resources, (3) rollback hooks register resource snapshotters and revert actions, and (4) RBAC/feature-flag gating prevents accidental live scheduling. This change als...

title: Support attaching retention config export to audit log evidence package
preview:
Context
Customers (and GRC internally) frequently request both (a) audit logs for a specified window and (b) an export of the tenant’s retention configuration (incl. defaults + overrides + change history pointer) as a single evidence package. This PR adds a first-class way to bundle the retention config export along...
text:
Context
Customers (and GRC internally) frequently request both (a) audit logs for a specified window and (b) an export of the tenant’s retention configuration (incl. defaults + overrides + change history pointer) as a single evidence package. This PR adds a first-class way to bundle the retention config export alongside the audit log export output.

Scope
- Add optional attachment support to evidence packages produced by audit-log-exporter.
- Implement a RetentionConfigAttachmentProvider which fetches retention config export from the control plane export endpoint and embeds it in the package.
- Update report template runner to allow "attachments" definitions (initially: retention_config).
- Add guardrails: size limits, explicit allowlist, and redaction check for known sensitive fields.
- Update CLI + HTTP API to accept the attachment flag(s).

Design notes
- Attachments are stored wit...

title: introduce-mini-deterministic-benchmark-corpus-and-sparse-cpu-probe-reporter
preview: Motivation: We lack a compact, deterministic benchmark fixture that reproduces common short-sequence hotpaths (latency-sensitive generations of length 8-64 tokens) while being cheap to run in CI and on local dev machines. Current suites are heavyweight and noisy; this PR introduces a curated mini corpus plus an expe...
text:
Motivation: We lack a compact, deterministic benchmark fixture that reproduces common short-sequence hotpaths (latency-sensitive generations of length 8-64 tokens) while being cheap to run in CI and on local dev machines. Current suites are heavyweight and noisy; this PR introduces a curated mini corpus plus an experimental sparse CPU probe reporter that samples kernel-level wall-clock for a small set of annotated hotspots. The goal is faster benches that still exercise scheduling, KV-cache priming, and transient batch assembly so we can catch regressions early.

What this PR contains (high-level):
- mini_bench_corpus/: a compact set (12 sessions) of synthetic and real prompt traces sampled from production anonymized logs, normalized for determinism (fixed tokenization seeds, pinned random seeds, and controlled primer lengths).
- runner/mini_runner.py: lightweight harness that spins u...

title: Expedited rejoin stabilizer and controlled load-trim hotfix
preview: Motivation: Following a recent outage reconstruction (SUP-4512) we observed an oscillation during regional rejoin where multiple nodes aggressively tried to reclaim tenant KV slots, triggering cascade redistributions and transient SLO violations. This PR introduces a short-term hotfix to: 1) introduce a gentle rejoi...
text: Motivation: Following a recent outage reconstruction (SUP-4512) we observed an oscillation during regional rejoin where multiple nodes aggressively tried to reclaim tenant KV slots, triggering cascade redistributions and transient SLO violations. This PR introduces a short-term hotfix to: 1) introduce a gentle rejoin backoff window (per-tenant jittered delay + exponential cap) to avoid synchronized reclaims; 2) add a controlled load-trim path that softly sheds low-priority traffic during rejoin windows; and 3) emit new telemetry to make oscillation and rejoin latency visible in the Console. This change is intentionally conservative: defaults apply only during detected rejoin epochs and are disabled outside of a rejoin recovery window. Changes: - runtime/scheduler/rejoin_coordinator.go: add rejoin state machine with randomized tenant backoff and exponential cooldown logic; add tests fo...

title: responses-adapter: propagate determinism/seed headers and structured-output annotations
preview: Motivation: The OpenAI Responses variants that customers adopt increasingly rely on explicit determinism/seed directives and lightweight structured-output annotations passed through the HTTP layer. Without preserving those hints across our OpenAI-compat adapter we observed divergences between direct upstream behavio...
text: Motivation: The OpenAI Responses variants that customers adopt increasingly rely on explicit determinism/seed directives and lightweight structured-output annotations passed through the HTTP layer. Without preserving those hints across our OpenAI-compat adapter we observed divergences between direct upstream behavior and Redwood-hosted responses in customer compatibility testing (see ENG-4821). This PR implements two coordinated changes in the adapter and runtime surface to improve compatibility and reduce customer surprises: 1) deterministic/seed propagation: accept and translate OpenAI-style determinism flags (seed, deterministic, x-seed) present as either JSON fields in the Responses body or as HTTP headers, and persist them through the routing, reassembly, and streaming layers so that streaming reassembly uses the same determinism hint used by the model selection logic; 2) structu...

title: holo-surge-sequencer-and-crossframe-occupancy-exporter
preview: Motivation: we observed sustained mid-tail p95 instability when replaying multi-sequence conversational traffic with interleaved priming and varied KV cache hits. Existing synthetic runners produce either Poisson-like bursts or deterministic ramps; they do not emulate the fractal-like, multi-scale surges we see in p...
text:
Motivation: we observed sustained mid-tail p95 instability when replaying multi-sequence conversational traffic with interleaved priming and varied KV cache hits. Existing synthetic runners produce either Poisson-like bursts or deterministic ramps; they do not emulate the fractal-like, multi-scale surges we see in production that cause cross-frame resource contention and transient kernel occupancy spikes. This PR introduces the 'holo-surge' sequencer (a compact workload generator that composes deterministic priming, stochastic microbursts, and long-period envelope modulation) and a crossframe occupancy exporter that records per-step GPU occupancy signatures and correlates them with logical request frames for downstream reporters.

What this change does: adds a new sequencer (cmd/holo-surge) and a reporting pipeline that serializes occupancy traces in a compact delta format compatible ...

title: Policy engine: compatibility guardrails for quantization profiles + safe fallback
preview: Motivation: Route-level quantization profile enablement must be safe-by-default. This PR adds compatibility evaluation inside the policy engine so that any resolved quantization profile ref is deterministically accepted/rejected based on (a) model family/variant constraints, (b) GPU SKU class support, and (c) max co...
text: Motivation: Route-level quantization profile enablement must be safe-by-default. This PR adds compatibility evaluation inside the policy engine so that any resolved quantization profile ref is deterministically accepted/rejected based on (a) model family/variant constraints, (b) GPU SKU class support, and (c) max context / sequence-length regime constraints. When a profile is incompatible, policy resolution falls back to baseline precision (i.e., quantization disabled) and emits explicit metrics/loggable reason codes; default behavior is unchanged when quantization is not enabled on the route. Scope: This change is in the control-plane policy resolution path (routing policy engine), not runtime kernel selection. The runtime still receives a resolved decision (apply profile X vs baseline). Design notes: (1) Determinism: compatibility evaluation uses only stable inputs available at poli...

title: openai-bridge: forward client RNG-hints and support structured-schema mapping for new Responses variants
preview: Summary: Add support in the OpenAI compatibility bridge for forwarding client-specified randomness hints and an explicit rng_seed field, and introduce structured-schema mapping to better handle the newer Responses API variants. This change ensures client requests that include 'randomness_hint' or 'rng_seed' are pres...
text: Summary: Add support in the OpenAI compatibility bridge for forwarding client-specified randomness hints and an explicit rng_seed field, and introduce structured-schema mapping to better handle the newer Responses API variants. This change ensures client requests that include 'randomness_hint' or 'rng_seed' are preserved through the bridge and honored by the serving runtime when possible, and maps new structured output descriptors from OpenAI-style Responses to Redwood's structured-output parser.

title: Bootstrap audit exporter service skeleton + CI + local dev harness
preview:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; ...
text:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; those will follow in subsequent PRs.

## Motivation / Context
We need a dedicated service to read audit events (initially via stubbed source in local dev), apply tenant scoping, and deliver to downstream destinations with retries/checkpointing. Before we can iterate on architecture and adapters, we need:
- Standard project layout + build tooling
- Baseline CI (lint/test/build, container build)
- A config model (typed) that can evolve with control-plane config sync
- A local harness so devs can run the service end-to-end without needing the full control plane

## What’s incl...

title: Add config sync from control plane (polling + streaming) with tenant scoping + caching
preview: Implements config sync from the control plane for the audit exporter service. This adds (1) tenant scoping so a given exporter instance only processes configs it is authorized to see, (2) a resilient polling loop with jitter/backoff and a long-poll “watch” mode when supported by the control plane, and (3) an in-memo...
text:
Implements config sync from the control plane for the audit exporter service. This adds (1) tenant scoping so a given exporter instance only processes configs it is authorized to see, (2) a resilient polling loop with jitter/backoff and a long-poll “watch” mode when supported by the control plane, and (3) an in-memory snapshot cache with safe defaults so we do not accidentally start exporting for a tenant unless the config is explicitly enabled.

Motivation
- ENG-5139 created the exporter scaffold; we need an end-to-end way to pull export destinations/filters from the control plane without requiring restarts.
- ENG-5132 is landing CRUD + validation in the control plane; this PR wires up the consumer side.

Key behaviors
- Startup: exporter boots in “no tenants enabled” state until it successfully fetches a config snapshot.
- Polling: periodic snapshot fetch (default 10s with +/-20% ji...

title: Filtering engine v1: allowlist/denylist by category/action/resource
preview: Implements filtering engine v1 for SIEM-ready audit export (ENG-5160). This adds a small, explicit rule model (allowlist/denylist) that can be evaluated per-event in the exporter with deterministic semantics and safe defaults. Scope in this PR: (1) filter model + evaluation logic (categories/actions/resource predica...
text: Implements filtering engine v1 for SIEM-ready audit export (ENG-5160). This adds a small, explicit rule model (allowlist/denylist) that can be evaluated per-event in the exporter with deterministic semantics and safe defaults. Scope in this PR: (1) filter model + evaluation logic (categories/actions/resource predicates), (2) strict config validation + normalization (including “deny wins” and invalid rule rejection), and (3) comprehensive unit tests for tricky cases (empty filters, conflicting allow/deny, unknown categories, wildcard handling, resource/action normalization, timestamp boundary behavior). Motivation: we need customer-configurable filtering that is auditable, easy to reason about during incident response, and stable enough to map to UI + control-plane API without frequent behavior changes. Semantics (as implemented): The effective policy is evaluated in this order: valida...

title: Route config: add quantization_profile_ref (default-preserving)
preview: Summary: This PR extends the route configuration schema to include an optional quantization_profile_ref field. When the field is unset (or explicitly set to "default"), behavior is unchanged and the serving stack will continue to use the baseline (non-profile) execution path. Motivation: The Quantization Profile Lib...
text: Summary: This PR extends the route configuration schema to include an optional quantization_profile_ref field. When the field is unset (or explicitly set to "default"), behavior is unchanged and the serving stack will continue to use the baseline (non-profile) execution path. Motivation: The Quantization Profile Library workstream needs a control-plane surface area to opt in per route (and later by region/tier) without changing tenant defaults. This PR is intentionally limited to route config wiring + compatibility-safe defaults; policy evaluation and runtime application are handled in follow-up PRs. Design notes: - New field: quantization_profile_ref (string). Allowed values in this PR are: unset/null, "default", or a concrete profile ref string (format validation in control plane comes later). - Default behavior: treat unset and "default" identically; downstream resolution returns "...

title: Emit metrics + tracing for Private backup/restore operations (with error categorization)
preview: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds...
text: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds: (1) structured Prometheus metrics for backup + restore operations (success/failure, duration, bytes, last success timestamps), (2) OpenTelemetry tracing spans with consistent attributes for per-step visibility, and (3) explicit error categorization to drive dashboards/alerts and reduce pager noise. Scope: This instruments the control-plane backup/restore path (installer-driven + control-plane endpoints) and adds common helpers used by both “backup create” and “restore apply”. It does not change backup artifact format or encryption behavior. Metrics (Prometheus): - redwood...

title: Private: expose backup status endpoints from control plane
preview: Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with ...
text:
Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with strict authz and safe redaction (no bucket names, object keys, or secret material). This unblocks Console UI + automated restore drills to gate on “recent successful backup” and to page on backup job regressions.

What’s included: Adds a BackupStatus read model persisted by the backup orchestrator after each run (success/failure), keyed by tenant_id and environment_id; Read-only endpoints under the Private API surface; Includes timestamps (last_success_at, last_attempt_at), status enum, last_error_category + last_error_message (truncated), artifact metadata (format_version,...

title: Add trafficctl emergency GSLB weight setter for api.redwoodinference.com
preview:
### Context
During game day and recent regional brownouts, the fastest safe mitigation is to shift Hosted API traffic between regions without waiting for Terraform applies. We currently do this via manual console operations, which is error-prone.

### What this PR does
- Introduces `trafficctl`, an audited CLI wrapp...
text:
### Context
During game day and recent regional brownouts, the fastest safe mitigation is to shift Hosted API traffic between regions without waiting for Terraform applies. We currently do this via manual console operations, which is error-prone.

### What this PR does
- Introduces `trafficctl`, an audited CLI wrapper around our GSLB provider API
- Adds commands to:
 - read current targets + weights
 - set weights for api.redwoodinference.com
 - set record TTL (temporary)
- Enforces RBAC via the `infra-traffic-admin` role
- Writes an audit log event for every invocation (who, what record, old/new weights)

### Usage
```
trafficctl gslb get --record api.redwoodinference.com
trafficctl dns set-ttl --record api.redwoodinference.com --ttl 60
trafficctl gslb set-weights --record api.redwoodinference.com --weights us-east-1-gw=0,us-west-2-gw=100
trafficctl dns set-ttl --record api.redwoodin...

title: Add sparse error annotator and CI autorepair hook for flaky prompt-sets
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'sparse error annotator' and an accompanying CI autorepair hook aimed at reducing noisy, low-density failures in prompt-set regression checks. The annotator detects when a regression signal is concentrated in very few prompts or is likely caused by brittle input formatting; ...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'sparse error annotator' and an accompanying CI autorepair hook aimed at reducing noisy, low-density failures in prompt-set regression checks. The annotator detects when a regression signal is concentrated in very few prompts or is likely caused by brittle input formatting; the autorepair hook runs a set of deterministic canonicalizers on flagged examples and re-executes the failing tests in CI. If the example passes post-repair the check is quarantined as a probable flake instead of failing the entire job. Motivation: We saw recurring alerts in canary / smoke evals where 1-2 prompts per prompt-set caused CI failures that were later determined to be formatting or tokenization edge-cases. These false positives interrupt rollouts and cause unnecessary investigations. This change reduces noisiness while preserving sensitivity to real regressions.

title: Fork-and-Join rollout controller with stable session affinity
preview: Summary: Introduce a new Fork-and-Join rollout controller and CRD (ForkJoinRollout) that allows operators to split traffic into multiple parallel rollout paths (forks), run independent validation windows, then atomically join a winning path back into the primary serving graph while preserving stable session affinity...
text: Summary: Introduce a new Fork-and-Join rollout controller and CRD (ForkJoinRollout) that allows operators to split traffic into multiple parallel rollout paths (forks), run independent validation windows, then atomically join a winning path back into the primary serving graph while preserving stable session affinity and KV cache locality. Motivation: Some experiments require running two or more independent model variants for overlapping cohorts and then merging the best-performing variant into production without user-visible churn or cache thrash. Existing canary and shadow primitives support linear progressive rollouts but do not provide first-class semantics for forked experiments and deterministic joins. This PR implements the control plane and runtime hooks to enable fork-and-join workflows with safety fences and rollback tracebacks. Changes: Commits: 1) Add CRD and API surface fo...

title: asymmetric-concurrency-skew-generator-and-delta-reporter
preview: Motivation: production traffic often exhibits heavy user skew where a small fraction of sessions produce high token bursts and long-lived KV cache pressure. Existing synthetic runners in benchmark-lab model uniform sequence mixes or simple hot-spot priors; we need a compact harness that can: 1) generate temporally c...
text:
Motivation: production traffic often exhibits heavy user skew where a small fraction of sessions produce high token bursts and long-lived KV cache pressure. Existing synthetic runners in benchmark-lab model uniform sequence mixes or simple hot-spot priors; we need a compact harness that can: 1) generate temporally correlated bursty sessions with configurable skew parameters; 2) drive the serving runtime under asymmetric concurrency to exercise scheduling and KV cache eviction behaviors; and 3) produce a compact delta report that correlates latency/regression deltas with kernel-level sample points and concurrency slices so that regressions can be triaged to scheduling vs kernel choices.

This PR implements: a) an "asymmetric-concurrency-skew" workload generator (bench/chaos/asymmetric_skew.go + python harness) supporting: percentiles-of-sessions that become "bursters", configurable bur...

title: credential-fuse-pausable-stream-lifecycle-tri-sdk-bump
preview: Summary: This PR implements a cross-language ergonomic surface for pausable/resumable streaming consumers, and a lightweight credential handoff mechanism we call 'credential-fuse' that reduces reauth churn during short-lived auth windows. Motivation: several teams reported latency spikes and redundant reauth calls d...
text: Summary: This PR implements a cross-language ergonomic surface for pausable/resumable streaming consumers, and a lightweight credential handoff mechanism we call 'credential-fuse' that reduces reauth churn during short-lived auth windows. Motivation: several teams reported latency spikes and redundant reauth calls during bursty streaming workloads; debugging showed frequent credential refresh races and lack of a clean pause/resume lifecycle on client streams. Goals: 1) provide pausable stream primitives that preserve local cursor/offset state, allow explicit credential handoff with minimal RPC, and provide telemetry for retry decisioning; 2) harmonize ergonomic APIs across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs so examples and migration paths are consistent; 3) surface retry hints and a small set of typed retry presets to make opt-in behavior explicit. What changed (high level): - SDK Python: adde...

title: Consensus-led A/B Failover with Explainable Decision Traces
preview: Motivation: Canary/A-B promotions in multi-tenant services often react to single-signal thresholds (latency or error rate). That produces noisy rollbacks and opaque decisions for downstream SREs and product owners. This change introduces a lightweight consensus-driven failover controller that aggregates orthogonal s...
text:
Motivation: Canary/A-B promotions in multi-tenant services often react to single-signal thresholds (latency or error rate). That produces noisy rollbacks and opaque decisions for downstream SREs and product owners. This change introduces a lightweight consensus-driven failover controller that aggregates orthogonal signals (p50/p95 latency deltas, availability/error spikes, semantic drift via embedding distance, and synthetic smoke checks) across cohorts to make an explainable pass/fail decision for A/B promotions and canaries.

What this PR does (summary): Adds a new controller (consensus_ab_controller) that: collects metric shards from multiple telemetry sources, computes a configurable quorum using weighted voters, produces a deterministic decision (+trace) for promote/rollback/freeze, and exposes the decision trace to the rollout logs and the Console. Includes a rehearsal harness f...
